summary: Split the sysdolphin/baselib hsd_3B34 JPEG range into hsd_3B34 and new hsd_3B5C translation units at hsd_803B5C4C, added large nonmatching C bodies and raw JPEG table data for both TUs, registered the new object in the build, and kept hsd_803B5C2C matching while improving surrounding fuzzy/data matches.

key_files.role: Changed section ownership so hsd_3B34 ends at text 0x803B5C4C/data 0x80431090/sdata2 0x804DEB90 and added new hsd_3B5C ranges through 0x803B7230/0x804316C0/0x804DEC00.

Expanded from the small hsd_803B5C2C stub into the first JPEG routine/table cluster, including hsd_803B3408, fn_803B376C, hsd_803B3CD8, hsd_803B46D4, hsd_803B4A2C, hsd_803B4D64, hsd_803B51C8, and hsd_803B5C2C.

New second TU beginning at hsd_803B5C4C with decode-side helper cluster evidence, large lbl_80431090 data table, lbl_80431638/80431678/80431690/8043169C/804316B4 tables, and functions through hsd_803B6BE4.
Added platform.h and typed u8/s32/u32 prototypes for hsd_3B34 and hsd_3B5C entry points.

what_changed: The old combined hsd_3B34 split range text 0x803B3408-0x803B7230 was cut into hsd_3B34 text 0x803B3408-0x803B5C4C and new hsd_3B5C text 0x803B5C4C-0x803B7230.

extab/extabindex were also split at adjacent boundaries: hsd_3B34 uses extab 0x80005668-0x80005690 and extabindex 0x800058AC-0x800058E8; hsd_3B5C uses extab 0x80005690-0x800056B0 and extabindex 0x800058E8-0x80005918.
Data ownership was separated: hsd_3B34 owns .data 0x80430C40-0x80431090 and .sdata2 0x804DEB10-0x804DEB90, while hsd_3B5C owns .data 0x80431090-0x804316C0 and .sdata2 0x804DEB90-0x804DEC00.

hsd_3B34.c gained nonmatching C and raw static JPEG tables, including lbl_803B9670 rodata, lbl_80430C40, lbl_80430C80, extern references to later tables, global state externs, Gecko_setjmp usage, placeholder.h, and M2C_FIELD-heavy buffer access.

hsd_3B5C.c was introduced as a new nonmatching C file with hsd_803B5C4C, hsd_803B5D70, hsd_803B5EA0, fn_803B61B4, fn_803B6820, hsd_803B6BE4, the lbl_80431090 table, and related zigzag/Huffman-style tables.
hsd_803B5C2C remained in hsd_3B34 as the end-of-first-cluster routine and still clamps its mode/arg0 to the 1..0xA range, defaulting lbl_804D6398 to 3.
The PR body reports fuzzy status of hsd_3B34 at 84.855255% and hsd_3B5C at 84.95146%, with verification via pre-commit and python configure.py --wrapper build/tools/wibo && ninja.
smart_moves: The TU split was justified with multiple independent section signals rather than a single function address: clean adjacent extab/extabindex cut points plus clean .data/.sdata2 table boundaries.
Keeping hsd_803B5C2C before the split exploited the fact that it matched 100% immediately before hsd_803B5C4C, supporting hsd_803B5C4C as the start of the next cluster.

Splitting around table ownership avoided forcing one TU to straddle two static JPEG table clusters: hsd_3B34 keeps the first cluster through lbl_80430C40/.sdata2 804DEB90, while hsd_3B5C starts at lbl_80431090.
Registering hsd_3B5C as NonMatching in configure.py allowed the repository to link and track fuzzy progress without pretending the large codec routines were fully solved.
Raw byte/halfword arrays and m2c-style field accesses preserved section layout and data matches while leaving semantic struct recovery for later work.
decomp_lessons: For suspected TU boundaries in library code, corroborate text splits with extab, extabindex, .data, and .sdata2 boundaries; this PR is a strong example of a split at hsd_803B5C4C backed by all of those signals.
A small 100% matching routine immediately before a candidate boundary can be useful evidence that the next function starts a new cluster.

Large codec decomp can make measurable progress by first landing accurate rodata/data tables and nonmatching C skeletons; the bot reported new 100% data/rodata matches even though functions remained fuzzy.
When semantic layouts are not known, temporary M2C_FIELD offsets and address-based labels are acceptable if they preserve matching progress and keep uncertainty visible.
Header cleanup should use project typedefs from platform.h for prototypes crossing TUs, but unknown functions should retain address-based names until real names are evidenced.
naming_conventions: Translation units are named from their starting address range, e.g. hsd_3B34.c and hsd_3B5C.c.
Unknown exported functions keep address names such as hsd_803B3408, hsd_803B51C8, hsd_803B5C4C, and hsd_803B6BE4.
File-local unknown helpers use fn_ address names, shown by fn_803B376C, fn_803B61B4, and fn_803B6820.
Unknown data keeps lbl_ address labels with explicit sizes/types, such as lbl_803B9670[0x1B6], lbl_80430C40[0x40], lbl_80431090[0x5A8], and lbl_80431638[0x40].
The header moved toward platform typedefs, using u8, s32, and u32 instead of plain int where prototypes were touched.
The decompiled signature for hsd_803B3408 names the unused fifth parameter unused_arg4, marking known unusedness without inventing semantics.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Used config/GALE01/splits.txt section boundaries as matching evidence and as the link layout source of truth for the new TU.
Used Object(NonMatching, "sysdolphin/baselib/hsd_3B5C.c") so the new file participates in builds while still being tracked as nonmatching.
Used placeholder.h and M2C_FIELD for unresolved struct/global-buffer offsets, especially around hsd_804D2648 and hsd_804D2E70.
Defined HSD_804D2648_BUF as a u8* cast of hsd_804D2648 to model byte-offset arithmetic over the jmp_buf-like global.
Included Gecko_setjmp.h and modeled error exits with __jmp_buf, __setjmp, and longjmp, matching the control-flow style visible in the generated C.
Landed exact raw JPEG/JFIF/Huffman/quantization-style tables as u8/u16 arrays to secure .rodata and .data matches before deeper cleanup.

review_feedback.feedback: Bot report for GALE01 said matched data rose to 39.28% (+0.26%, +3126 bytes) with 3 new matches: hsd_3B5C .data +1584, hsd_3B34 .data +1104, and hsd_3B34 .rodata +438.
The same report listed 15 improvements in unmatched items, including hsd_803B3CD8, hsd_803B51C8, fn_803B61B4, fn_803B376C, hsd_803B6BE4, hsd_803B4D64, hsd_803B5C4C, and hsd_803B5D70.
No human reviews or review comments were present in the provided slice.

follow_up_queries: Find later PRs that replace M2C_FIELD or HSD_804D2648_BUF usage in hsd_3B34.c or hsd_3B5C.c with real structs.
Find later PRs making hsd_803B51C8, hsd_803B6BE4, hsd_803B5C4C, fn_803B61B4, or fn_803B6820 fully matching.
Find other TU split PRs that used extab/extabindex plus .data/.sdata2 boundaries as evidence.
Find PRs that assign semantic names to the HAL Laboratory/JFIF/JPEG tables lbl_803B9670, lbl_80430C40, lbl_80431090, or lbl_80431638.
Find follow-up build/config changes that move hsd_3B34.c or hsd_3B5C.c from NonMatching to Matching.
